title: VulnHunter scan manifest
description: Authoritative agent-to-scan-worker contract written at <results_dir>/scan_manifest.json on every run that produces a results directory (agent exit codes 0, 1, 3, 4, or 5). Schema_version bumps are explicit and breaking; the agent validates before writing and the scan-worker validates on read.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Constant for this schema file; consumers fail-closed if it differs.

scan_id.description: Per-scan identifier. Equals the results-directory basename: <repo>_VULNHUNT_RESULTS_<model>_<UTC-timestamp>.

agent_exit_code.description: Process exit code the agent will return. 0=success, 1=no findings, 2=publish failed, 3=partial issue post, 4=agent crash. Code 2 is included for completeness even though the manifest is normally absent in that path.

cost_usd.description: Cumulative USD cost the agent observed for this run, as reported by the Claude Agent SDK ResultMessage (running-max across continuations).

findings.description: Confirmed findings extracted from the scan README. Empty array is valid (exit code 1).

posted.description: GitHub issues the agent created during the issues stage.

skipped.description: Findings the agent deduplicated against pre-existing open issues on the target repo.

failed.description: Findings the agent attempted to post but the GitHub API errored.

severity.description: Severity rating; empty string is allowed when the extractor could not determine it.

cwe.description: CWE identifier (CWE-NNN); empty string allowed when not determined.

vulnfix_key.description: 16-char lowercase hex SHA-256 prefix over (location, cwe, root_cause). Stable across scans so re-runs collide on the same vulnerability.

github_issue_url.description: GitHub issue URL. Permits enterprise hosts (e.g. a GitHub Enterprise instance) — scan-worker parses owner/repo/number via regex. HTTPS-only: GitHub's REST `html_url` is always https on both github.com and GHEC, so a real producer should never emit http://; rejecting it removes a small inconsistency with the schema's fail-closed posture and narrows a hypothetical auth-token-leak risk if any future scan-worker code path follows the URL with credentials attached.

title.description: Short human-readable title; no CWE prefix, no severity, no VULN-id.

cwe_name.description: Human-readable CWE name (e.g. "SQL Injection"). Empty string allowed when not determined.

location.description: Primary code location, typically "src/path/file.py:42". Empty string allowed when not determined.

root_cause.description: One-sentence description of the underlying defect.

entry_point.description: HTTP route, function, or other public surface where the vulnerability is reachable.

exploit_description.description: What an attacker can do (1-3 sentences). Describe behavior, not exploit code.

exploit_impact.description: Concrete consequence (data disclosure, RCE, account takeover, etc.).

fix_strategy.description: One-sentence summary of the proposed fix from the report.

poc_path.description: Relative path to the PoC file inside the results directory, or null when absent.

exploit_test_path.description: Relative path to the exploit-test file inside the results directory, or null when absent.

matched_issue_numbers.description: Open GitHub issue numbers that already track this finding (vulnfix-key dedup or semantic-title match).

via.description: Dedup mechanism that matched: "key" (vulnfix_key marker found in an existing issue body), "semantic" (title similarity), or "" when unspecified.

error.description: Diagnostic message from the GitHub API error or local exception.
